summary: react-wp-viewer postinstall beacons to hardcoded bare IP confirming dependency-confusion landing
details: react-wp-viewer@0.2.99 ships a postinstall.js that, on every install, performs an unconditional HTTP GET to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase carrying the package name, version, and a fixed nonce as query parameters. The path segment 'dc-20260627-yandex-geobase' self-identifies the package as a dependency-confusion probe targeting an internal/private name (likely 'yandex-geobase'). The beacon's purpose is to notify the operator of the bare-IP endpoint whenever a victim build system resolves this public name in place of an intended internal package, identifying exploitable targets for a follow-on malicious release. Although the current payload is limited to install metadata (no env vars, filesystem, or credential reads), the package's only behavior on install is reconnaissance to attacker-controlled infrastructure, and the namespace presence itself causes installer harm by hijacking the intended internal dependency.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
